evaluation.summary: The Shapley value's coefficient of variation rises sharply with heavier-tailed player weight distributions, reaching 0.82 for Dirichlet weights, revealing strong sensitivity to weight variability.
next_directions: Extend analysis to larger player sets and cooperative structures beyond weight randomness.
Derive analytical bounds linking weight distribution moments to Shapley value variance.
Compare Shapley variance with alternative solution concepts (e.g., nucleolus, core) under stochastic weights.
